headline: Micron (MU) Q3 FY26 SHATTERS records — $41.5B rev (+346% YoY) vs $34.66B cons, GM 84.9% (vs 81.6% cons), $25.11 adj EPS vs $19.95 cons; Q4 GUIDE $50B revenue / $30-32 EPS / 85% GM TURNS THE PRINT INTO A STRUCTURAL RE-RATE; 16 SCAs WORTH ~$100B MIN COMMITMENT through 2030; stock +14.6% AH to ~$1,190
summary: Micron reported fiscal Q3 2026 results after Tuesday's close that obliterated every Wall Street acid test. Revenue $41.5B (+346% YoY) vs $34.66B consensus (+$6.84B beat); non-GAAP gross margin 84.9% vs 81.6% consensus — the highest in MU's 47-year history and well clear of the 80% cycle-cresting bear threshold; non-GAAP diluted EPS $25.11 vs $19.95 cons (+$5.16 beat, +106% sequentially). Q4 guidance is the STRUCTURAL TURN: revenue ~$50B vs ~$36B Street, EPS $30-32 vs ~$24.80 cons, GM ~85% (modest 'rate of price increases' moderation flagged — the only soft datapoint in the print). HBM4 detail: >$1B in HBM4 revenue shipped in Q3, 12-high ramp tracking 2x faster than HBM3E 12-high; HBM3E AND HBM4 NOW FULLY BOOKED THROUGH CALENDAR 2027 with demand visible into 2028. Most important strategic disclosure: 16 Strategic Customer Agreements (SCAs) signed across datacenter/consumer/automotive, 14 of them with cumulative minimum revenue commitments of ~$100 BILLION through end of CY2030 — take-or-pay, non-cancellable, with $22B in upfront cash deposits/financial commitments back-end-loaded. Stock reaction: MU +14.6% AH to ~$1,190 (extended above $1,200 intraday). Read-through to House coverage: (1) MU IS THE STRUCTURAL WIN OF THE AI-MEMORY CYCLE — the $100B SCA disclosure reframes MU from cyclical-DRAM-trader to long-duration AI-infra contract-revenue company; this is the analog to AVGO's custom-silicon LOC roll-forward; (2) STRONG positive read-through to SK Hynix (000660.KS) and Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) — HBM3E/HBM4 sold-out confirmation removes ALL credible cycle-cresting bear cases through 2027; (3) MASSIVE positive for memory-WFE — Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), KLA (KLAC), ASML, Tokyo Electron (8035.T) — the structural $100B SCA roll-forward forces capex visibility into FY27-FY28 prints; (4) downstream positive for ANET, AVGO, MRVL on HBM-bandwidth-fed networking demand; (5) confirms NVDA Vera Rubin and Vera Rubin Ultra silicon roadmap timing — MU's HBM4 ramp is on schedule.

headline: KOSPI SURGES +5.42% to ~8,930 on MU print — SK Hynix (000660.KS) +13% to record high, Samsung (005930.KS) +5%; Korea Exchange briefly halts program trading at +5% open trip; Nikkei joins; Kioxia (285A.T) +12%; KOSPI 9,000 now in reach as memory-supercycle bear-case officially killed
summary: The KOSPI rallied +5.42% to ~8,930 Thursday on the back of Micron's Q3 FY26 blowout. SK Hynix Inc surged +13% (some prints near +13.5%) to a fresh all-time record high — investors reacted to (a) MU's HBM3E/HBM4 sold-out-through-CY27 disclosure validating Hynix's own HBM order book and (b) confirmation of the formal $29.4B Nasdaq ADR listing tentatively July 10. Samsung Electronics +5% as the MU print read-through neutralized lingering Foundry/HBM4-lag concerns; Samsung's KRW 90T buyback announced Wednesday continues providing technical bid. Kioxia (285A.T) Tokyo +12% sympathetic, Tokyo Electron (8035.T) +7%+ on memory-WFE pull-through. The Korea Exchange briefly halted program trading after the index jumped >5% at the open. Index now ~70 points from KOSPI 9,000 — the post-Lehman psychological level no Korea-equity bear had on the bingo card 60 days ago. Net foreign flow turned aggressively positive after Tuesday's KRW 5.79T historical outflow. Read-through to House coverage: (1) THE BREADTH CRACK FROM JUNE 19 IS NOW FULLY REPAIRED — the AI-memory technical correction took only 4 sessions to round-trip; (2) the V-shape signals Korea is again the levered beta to U.S. AI-infra catalysts — front-running NVDA's Vera Rubin shipment cycle; (3) confirms Wednesday's call that the Samsung KRW 90T buyback formed a real technical floor; (4) all Korean memory-supply-chain names (KOSPI listed: 000660.KS, 005930.KS) now have BOTH passive Korea-flow and U.S. ADR-flow tailwinds; (5) sympathetically positive for U.S. AI-infra at open — Nasdaq futures +0.8%.

headline: WALL ST PRICE TARGET SCRAMBLE on MU post-print — JPMorgan $1,540 (from $550), Susquehanna $2,000 (from $1,750), Barclays $2,000 (from $1,175), DA Davidson $2,000 (from $1,500); Goldman Sachs (only bear at $400) under pressure — consensus average rolls to ~$1,540, top-end now ~$2,000
summary: Following Micron's record Q3 print Tuesday after close, Wall Street price targets were dramatically reset upward Wednesday evening and Thursday pre-market. JPMorgan analyst Harlan Sur immediately raised the Overweight-rated PT to $1,540 (from $550) — citing the Q3 rev/GM/EPS triple-beat plus the $100B SCA structural disclosure. Susquehanna's Mehdi Hosseini took his PT to $2,000 (from $1,750). Barclays' Tom O'Malley moved to $2,000 (from $1,175) — a ~70% PT hike in one print. DA Davidson moved to $2,000 (from $1,500). Bank of America had pre-positioned at $1,500 (June 23) and Needham at $1,550 (June 22). Goldman Sachs — the sole credible bear at ~$400 PT — under acute desk pressure post-print; GS commentary now acknowledges 'long-term agreements will help raise the P/E multiple based on peak earnings,' a near-capitulation tone. New consensus average price target rolls toward ~$1,540; top-end now $2,000 from three independent firms. Read-through to House coverage: (1) the implied EPS power supporting $2,000 PTs at ~25-30x multiple is $66-80/share — implies FY27 EPS run-rate of ~$110-130 vs current consensus ~$100; (2) MU now joins NVDA, AVGO as the third AI-infra name with a fully-converged consensus bull case; (3) sympathetically positive for SK Hynix, Samsung — Korean memory analyst PT scrambles follow today; (4) positive sentiment for AMAT, LRCX, KLAC on memory-WFE capex outlook ahead of Q2 prints.

headline: POST-CCAR CAPITAL RETURN WAVE — JPM unveils $50B BUYBACK + div +10% to $1.65; GS dividend +11% to $5.00; MS dividend +15% to $1.15 + $20B BUYBACK reauthorization; all 32 banks pass severely-adverse scenario; SCB unchanged through Sept 2027
summary: The Federal Reserve released 2026 Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR) results Wednesday at 4 PM ET. All 32 large bank holding companies remained above minimum capital requirements even under the severely-adverse scenario (10% unemployment, -30% home prices, -39% CRE, $708B projected industry losses). Capital-return announcements followed within hours: (a) JPMorgan Chase — NEW $50B share repurchase program effective July 1, quarterly dividend +10% to $1.65 (from $1.50); (b) Goldman Sachs — dividend +11% to $5.00; (c) Morgan Stanley — dividend +15% to $1.15, $20B buyback reauthorization; (d) Wells Fargo — dividend +11% to $0.50; (e) Bank of America (announced previously, July 2025 cycle) — dividend +8% to $0.28. Critically, the SCB (Stress Capital Buffer) requirements REMAIN UNCHANGED through September 30, 2027 while the Fed reviews its stress-test models — so the CCAR-as-capital-binding constraint has been neutralized for ~15 months. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NOT a direct AI-Datacenter-Power name catalyst, but a meaningful capital-flow positive — bank buybacks of $50-100B+ across the cohort create flow-of-funds support broadly; (2) marginally positive for software AI-infra (PLTR, NOW, CRM) and high-multiple growth — bank-stock multiple expansion historically correlates with high-beta growth bid; (3) modestly positive for credit-sensitive AI-infra capex (CRWV, NBIS) — easier corporate-bond conditions; (4) the SCB freeze through 9/2027 means the SCB is no longer a binding constraint on bank lending capacity — supports the AI-infra capex thesis through 2027.

headline: POWELL DAY 2 TESTIMONY — Senate Banking Committee Wednesday afternoon; restates patience on cuts pending June-July CPI; commits to full term despite Trump pressure; CME July-cut probability HOLDS at 18.8% (modest hawkish drift vs whisper-bar); September cut probability ~62%
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell completed Day 2 of his semi-annual Monetary Policy Report Wednesday afternoon before the Senate Banking Committee. Key incremental headlines vs Wednesday's House testimony: (1) Powell explicitly committed to serving out his full term — pushed back against Trump-administration pressure to resign; (2) restated 'the Fed is well-positioned to learn more about price increases before changing interest rates'; (3) flagged June and July CPI prints as the key gate to the first cut; (4) acknowledged most FOMC participants support cutting rates this year, but consensus is to wait for tariff pass-through clarity. Market reaction: CME FedWatch July-cut probability held at 18.8% (unchanged from Tuesday post-House testimony); September probability rose marginally to ~62%; December terminal expectations little changed. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MODESTLY NEGATIVE for long-duration utility/SMR names (CEG, VST, GEV, OKLO, SMR) that price the rate-cut path most aggressively; (2) marginally negative for high-multiple software (PLTR, NOW, CRM) on duration sensitivity; (3) NEUTRAL for hyperscaler/AI-infra capex names — MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META capex is structurally rates-insensitive at $700B+ aggregate; (4) the tariff pass-through commentary remains a yellow flag for NVDA / AMD margin commentary into FQ2 prints — Section 232 25% advanced-chip-export duties not yet fully reflected in Street models.

headline: MEMORY-WFE READS THROUGH — Tokyo Electron (8035.T) +7%+ Tokyo; ASML / AMAT / LRCX / KLAC pre-market +2-3%; structural visibility into 2027-2028 capex on $100B MU SCA + HBM4 ramp through CY27; Tokyo Electron earlier guided FY26 capex +48%, raised FY operating profit to JPY 593B
summary: Wafer-fab equipment (WFE) names rallied sharply Thursday on the read-through from Micron's $100B SCA disclosure and HBM4-fully-booked-through-2027 commentary. Tokyo Electron (8035.T) +7%+ Tokyo close, building on its earlier disclosed FY26 capex hike of +48% to a record high and revised FY operating profit guide to JPY 593B (citing rapid AI-server / memory-equipment demand). ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX), KLA (KLAC) all up 2-3% pre-market. Structural setup: (1) the MU 14-of-16 SCA disclosure with ~$100B minimum take-or-pay revenue commitment through CY2030 forces memory makers to commit upfront wafer-capacity capex — visibility now extends 18-24 months further than Street models had assumed; (2) Tokyo Electron earlier indicated DRAM etching demand is the single-largest FY26 incremental driver — directly read-through to MU/Hynix HBM die-stacking equipment orders; (3) HBM4 requires more capacity-intensive logic-process steps (hybrid bonding, CoWoS-L-style packaging) — TSMC's CoWoS expansion projected +60%+ from end-2025 to end-2026 confirms the packaging-capex visibility. Read-through to House coverage: (1) DIRECTLY POSITIVE for AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, ASML, 8035.T, ENTG, AEHR, ASX, AMKR, 4063.T (Toray) — all memory-WFE / advanced-packaging names; (2) confirms the Tuesday CRWV/NBIS bull case — neocloud capex commits depend on supply-side capacity visibility, which the MU print just delivered; (3) positive for ENTG (specialty materials for HBM stacks); (4) FY27 capex outlook is now MORE bullish than pre-print expectations.

headline: HYPERSCALER 2026 CAPEX RATIFIES AT ~$725B — Microsoft $190B, Amazon $200B, Google $175-185B, Meta $115-125B; MU's $100B SCA disclosure VALIDATES the procurement chain; Microsoft remains capacity-constrained through 2026 despite +25% capex hike
summary: The Micron Q3 print contextualizes the calendar-2026 hyperscaler capex stack now standing at ~$725B aggregate across MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META — up ~77% from CY25 — and explains where the demand for MU's $100B in 14-SCA committed-revenue is going: (a) Microsoft CY2026 capex $190B vs $152B avg estimate, with management explicitly stating capacity-constrained through at least 2026; (b) Amazon CY2026 capex ~$200B vs ~$125B CY25; (c) Google CY2026 capex $175-185B vs $91B CY25 — nearly doubling; (d) Meta CY2026 capex $115-125B vs ~$85B CY25; (e) Oracle (per Jefferies 'industry sold out for any capacity within next 6 months' Tues commentary). MU's HBM3E/HBM4 fully-booked-through-CY27 disclosure plus the $100B SCA commitments are the supply-side mirror of these capex commits. Read-through to House coverage: (1) ALL hyperscalers covered (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, META) get their capex thesis reinforced — buy-the-dip on any rate-driven pullback; (2) STRONGLY positive for NVDA (Blackwell + Vera Rubin) and AVGO (custom-silicon LOC) — these capex dollars must monetize at GPU/custom-silicon procurement; (3) DIRECTLY positive for the AI-power supply chain: VRT, ETN, GEV (Eaton Q1 DC orders +240%), GLW (Corning +10.5% mid-day this week); (4) positive for nuclear / utility cohort CEG, VST, NEE, AES — the 725B capex implies 60-80 GW of incremental power demand through 2027 that grid build-out must serve.

headline: AI-POWER COHORT PRE-MARKET — VRT, ETN, GEV, GLW, ANET all +1-3%; Corning (GLW) rallied +10.5% Wednesday to fresh 52-week high $217.09; sympathy to MU print + Jefferies 'sold out 6 months' Oracle commentary supports the bottleneck-trade thesis
summary: The datacenter-power / picks-and-shovels cohort opens Thursday with strong sympathy bid on the Micron print + Wednesday's Oracle commentary that 'industry is sold out for any capacity within the next 6 months.' Key pre-market moves: Vertiv Holdings (VRT) +2%, Eaton (ETN) +2%, GE Vernova (GEV) +2-3%, Corning (GLW) flat-to-up after its +10.5% Wednesday surge to a fresh 52-week high of $217.09, Arista (ANET) +2% on HBM-bandwidth-fed networking read-through. Backlog context: Vertiv backlog stands at $15B (+109% YoY) per latest disclosure; Eaton Q1 2026 datacenter orders +240% YoY and DC revenue +50% with $22.8B backlog; GE Vernova Q1 orders exceeded all of last year. Vertiv completed the ThermoKey S.p.A. acquisition June 12 to expand thermal management for EMEA AI factories. Read-through to House coverage: (1) the AI-power bottleneck thesis just got triple-validation in 36 hours: (i) Jefferies 'sold-out' commentary on OCI capacity, (ii) MU's $100B SCA reinforcing the demand-side procurement chain, (iii) the hyperscaler $725B CY26 capex stack; (2) the picks-and-shovels names (VRT, ETN, GEV, GLW) increasingly look like the LOW-BETA way to express the AI-infra thesis vs high-multiple software (PLTR, NOW); (3) modestly negative for the SMR/nuclear cohort (OKLO, SMR, LEU) post-Powell hawkish-drift testimony — but structural buildout demand remains intact.
